description: You are a first-line support engineer who has been asked to collect error diagnostics from a small application log. Everything you need is already under /home/user/support. Goal 1. Inspect the file /home/user/support/logs/app.log. 2. Filter the log so that **only** the lines matching ALL of the following conditions are kept: • The line begins with the fixed date text 2023-10-15 (no other date is valid). • Somewhere in the line the exact uppercase word ERROR appears. • The line also contains a request identifier in the form REQ-NNNNNN where N is any digit (six digits total). (A single extended regular expression can satisfy this, but use whatever CLI tools you prefer.) 3. Preserve the original order of those lines and write them, **verbatim**, to a new plain-text file /home/user/support/diagnostics/errors.log. 4. After the last copied line, append a single new line that contains ONLY: TOTAL_ERRORS=<number_of_error_lines> For example, if three lines matched, the very last line of the file must literally be TOTAL_ERRORS=3 5. Also create a tiny collection marker file /home/user/support/diagnostics/collection.log with exactly two lines: line 1: collector=regex_filter line 2: status=success Format rules that the automated test will check: • errors.log must contain ONLY the matching log lines plus the final summary line. • The summary line must be the very last line in the file and must not have leading or trailing spaces. • The count in TOTAL_ERRORS=... must equal the number of preceding lines in that same file. • collection.log must consist of exactly the two lines stated above, terminated by a single newline at the end of the second line. Hints You will probably only need 2–3 commands (e.g., mkdir, grep/awk, shell redirection). Do not modify the original app.log; only read from it. All paths are writable by the current user, and you do NOT need super-user privileges. When finished, you can run cat /home/user/support/diagnostics/errors.log and cat /home/user/support/diagnostics/collection.log to confirm the results.
truth:
Initial setup (already present before the agent starts):

1. Directory structure & permissions  
   /home/user/support/ (0755)  
   /home/user/support/logs/ (0755)  

2. File: /home/user/support/logs/app.log (0644)  
   Contents (7 lines, each terminated by a single LF):  
   2023-10-15 10:15:42,123 INFO  main      REQ-000101 Starting service  
   2023-10-15 10:16:05,675 ERROR main      REQ-000102 Failed to connect to database  
   2023-10-15 10:16:07,101 WARN  worker-1  REQ-000103 Retrying  
   2023-10-15 10:16:10,333 ERROR worker-1  REQ-000104 Timeout reached during operation  
   2023-10-15 10:16:12,890 INFO  main      REQ-000105 Health check passed  
   2023-10-15 10:16:15,200 ERROR worker-2  REQ-000106 Null pointer exception  
   2023-10-15 10:16:16,400 INFO  worker-2  REQ-000107 Shutdown complete  

Expected artefacts after successful agent execution:

A. Directory /home/user/support/diagnostics/ (should exist, 0755 or stricter)

B. File: /home/user/support/diagnostics/errors.log (0644)  
   Exact contents (4 lines):  
   2023-10-15 10:16:05,675 ERROR main      REQ-000102 Failed to connect to database  
   2023-10-15 10:16:10,333 ERROR worker-1  REQ-000104 Timeout reached during operation  
   2023-10-15 10:16:15,200 ERROR worker-2  REQ-000106 Null pointer exception  
   TOTAL_ERRORS=3  

C. File: /home/user/support/diagnostics/collection.log (0644)  
   Exact contents (2 lines):  
   collector=regex_filter  
   status=success  

The automated grader will:
1. Verify the presence and readability of both files in /home/user/support/diagnostics/.  
2. Confirm that errors.log contains exactly three ERROR lines from app.log followed by the summary line TOTAL_ERRORS=3.  
3. Confirm that collection.log contains exactly the two specified lines with a newline at the end of the second line.  
4. Ensure no extra files were created inside /home/user/support/diagnostics/.
